events.text: Jeffrey Brown, 53-year-old Asian male, Former smoker (quit 2 years ago). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.

Diagnosed with Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the Base of tongue. Clinical staging: T3N1M0, AJCC Stage III. ICD-10: C10.9. Stage III HPV-positive squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx.
MRI neck with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated definitive concurrent chemoradiation: Cisplatin 100 mg/m2 q3w x 3 cycles concurrent with radiation. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated definitive concurrent chemoradiation: Cisplatin 100 mg/m2 q3w x 3 cycles concurrent with radiation. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 2 xerostomia. Pilocarpine 5 mg TID initiated. Artificial saliva recommended.
Initiated definitive concurrent chemoradiation: Cisplatin 100 mg/m2 q3w x 3 cycles concurrent with radiation. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
PET/CT performed for restaging after definitive concurrent chemoradiation.
Clinical visit for results review after definitive concurrent chemoradiation.
Completed definitive radiation: IMRT 70 Gy in 35 fractions to primary site and involved nodes, 56 Gy to elective nodal volumes. Treatment delivered as planned.
Clinical visit for post-radiation assessment.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
MRI NECK WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Brown
MRN: patient_e79e015cb2b8
Date of Study: 2022-02-12

CLINICAL INDICATION: Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the base of tongue. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: MRI performed with and without IV gadolinium contrast, multiplanar multisequence technique.

FINDINGS:
OROPHARYNX: Enhancing mass centered in the base of tongue measuring 4.3 x 3.0 cm with extension to the vallecula.
NECK: Level IIA lymphadenopathy on the right, largest node 2.5 cm with necrotic center.

IMPRESSION:
1. Oropharyngeal mass with cervical lymphadenopathy. Clinical staging consistent with Stage III.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Brown
MRN: patient_e79e015cb2b8
Specimen ID: S2022-44250
Date of Procedure: 2022-02-16
Date of Report: 2022-02-20

SPECIMEN: Base of tongue, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated), moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 5.4 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
p16: Positive (strong, diffuse)
p63: Positive
CK5/6: Positive
EBV: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Jeffrey Brown - Base of tongue" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 5.4 x 3.8 x 2.7 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A11.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

Electronically signed by: Sarah Chen, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Radiation Oncology

Patient: Jeffrey Brown
MRN: patient_e79e015cb2b8
Date: 2022-02-19
Provider: Dr. Amanda Foster

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Brown is a 53-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue, III. Diagnosis made following evaluation of progressive symptoms. Stage III HPV-positive squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx. Pathology confirmed squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated). Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports abdominal discomfort and early satiety.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- obstructive sleep apnea

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- No prior surgeries

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Never smoker
- Alcohol: Former heavy drinker, quit 5 years ago
- Occupation: Office manager
- Lives with spouse and adult children

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Mother with breast cancer at age 62.

ALLERGIES: Iodine contrast (anaphylaxis)

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: dry mouth, dizziness. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 112/88, HR 68, RR 17, Temp 98.3F, SpO2 95% on RA, Wt 65.0 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 4.6, Hgb 13.0, Plt 115, Cr 1.05

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
53-year-old male with III squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue. initial oncology consultation.

1. Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the base of tongue, Stage III
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend definitive concurrent chemoradiation
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Peripheral access adequate
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Helen Park, MD
Radiation Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Brown
MRN: patient_e79e015cb2b8
Date of Report: 2022-03-07
Specimen: Base of tongue (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated)
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (324 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 7.4 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  HPV HPV-16 positive (p16 IHC positive) - Viral [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  NOTCH1 p.P2164fs - Frameshift (Exon 34)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CASP8 p.R127* - VUS
  FAT1 p.G4551S - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - HPV-positive disease: Generally favorable prognosis. De-escalation trials may be appropriate.
  - PIK3CA mutation: Alpelisib under investigation in HNSCC.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 698x coverage.
Tumor content: 61%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Medical Oncology

Patient: Jeffrey Brown
MRN: patient_e79e015cb2b8
Date: 2022-04-03
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

CHIEF COMPLAINT: cycle 2 assessment

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Brown is a 53-year-old male with III squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue who presents for cycle 2 assessment. Patient has some concerns about side effects. Weight is stable.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: night sweats, decreased appetite, intermittent headaches, mild dyspnea on exertion. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 116/65, HR 82, RR 15, Temp 98.1F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 88.9 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 9.2, Hgb 9.1, Plt 266, Cr 0.89

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
53-year-old male with III squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue. cycle 2 assessment.

1. Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) - cycle 2 assessment
   - Proceed with next cycle as planned
   - Labs today show adequate counts for treatment
2. Symptom management
   - Neuropathy being monitored
3. Follow-up in 6 weeks

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD
Medical Oncology

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Brown
MRN: patient_e79e015cb2b8
Date of Study: 2022-05-19

CLINICAL INDICATION: Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the base of tongue. restaging after definitive concurrent chemoradiation.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Oropharyngeal mass with cervical lymphadenopathy. Clinical staging consistent with Stage III.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.

LABORATORY RESULTS:

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
53-year-old male with III squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue. results review after definitive concurrent chemoradiation.
